title: Fluid separation using immersed hydrophilic and oleophilic ribbons
abstract: An apparatus for separating fluids and controlling flow of production fluid includes a diversion assembly configured to receive production fluid produced from a subterranean region, the diversion assembly including an inflow conduit in fluid communication with a first outflow conduit and a second outflow conduit, the first outflow conduit in fluid communication with a surface location. The apparatus also includes a separation assembly including a hydrophilic ribbon and an oleophilic ribbon configured to be immersed in the production fluid, at least part of the hydrophilic ribbon and the oleophilic ribbon configured to move with the production fluid, the oleophilic ribbon configured to redirect a flow of hydrocarbons into the first outflow conduit, and the hydrophilic ribbon configured to redirect a flow of water-based fluid into the second outflow conduit.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus for separating fluids and controlling flow of production fluid 28, comprising: a diversion assembly configured to receive production fluid 28 produced from a subterranean region, the diversion assembly including an inflow conduit 33 in fluid communication with a first outflow conduit 30 and a second outflow conduit 32, the first outflow conduit 30 in fluid communication with a surface location; and a separation assembly including a hydrophilic ribbon 60 and an oleophilic ribbon 62 configured to be immersed in the production fluid 28, at least part of the hydrophilic ribbon 60 and the oleophilic ribbon 62 configured to move with the production fluid 28, the oleophilic ribbon 62 configured to redirect a flow of hydrocarbons into the first outflow conduit 30, and the hydrophilic ribbon 60 configured to redirect a flow of water-based fluid into the second outflow conduit 32. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the hydrophilic ribbon 60 is configured to attract water-based fluid and guide the water-based fluid in a viscous boundary layer toward the first outflow conduit 30, and the oleophilic ribbon 62 is configured to attract hydrocarbons and guide hydrocarbons in a viscous boundary layer toward the second outflow conduit 32 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein at least part of the oleophilic ribbon 62 is disposed proximate to an entrance of the first outflow conduit 30, and at least part of the hydrophilic ribbon 60 is disposed proximate to an entrance of the second outflow conduit 32. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the hydrophilic ribbon 60 has a first end fixedly disposed relative to the inflow conduit 33 and a second end extending into a fluid volume, and the oleophilic ribbon 62 has a first end fixedly disposed relative to the inflow conduit 33 and a second end extending into a fluid volume. 5. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the hydrophilic ribbon 60 is attached at the inflow conduit 33 and extends into the second outflow conduit 32, and the oleophilic ribbon 62 is attached at the inflow conduit 33 and extends into the first outflow conduit 30. 6. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the hydrophilic ribbon 60 includes a plurality of hydrophilic ribbon 60s 40 arrayed along a first side of the inflow conduit 33, and the oleophilic ribbon 62 includes a plurality of oleophilic ribbon 62s 42 arrayed along a second side of the inflow conduit 33 opposite to the first side. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the hydrophilic ribbon 60 is configured to adhere to water-based fluid in the production fluid 28, and the oleophilic ribbon 62 is configured to adhere to hydrocarbons in the production fluid 28. 8. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the hydrophilic ribbon 60 includes a plurality of hydrophilic ribbon 60s 40, and the oleophilic ribbon 62 includes a plurality of oleophilic ribbon 62s 42. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein each of the plurality of oleophilic ribbon 62s 42 includes a first end fixedly disposed relative to the inflow conduit 33 and a second end configured to be guided by the production fluid 28 into the first outflow conduit 30, and each of the plurality of hydrophilic ribbon 60s 40 includes a first end fixedly disposed relative to the inflow conduit 33 and a second end configured to be guided by the production fluid 28 into the second outflow conduit 32. 10. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein one or more of the hydrophilic and oleophilic ribbon 62s 42 are configured as thin flexible members having flat surfaces. 11. A method 100 of separating fluids and controlling flow of production fluid 28, comprising: disposing a fluid production apparatus in a second in a subterranean region, the apparatus including a diversion assembly having an inflow conduit 33 in fluid communication with a first outflow conduit 30 and a second outflow conduit 32, the first outflow conduit 30 in fluid communication with a surface location, the apparatus including a separation assembly having a hydrophilic ribbon 60 and an oleophilic ribbon 62 configured to be immersed in the production fluid 28, at least part of the hydrophilic ribbon 60 and the oleophilic ribbon 62 configured to move with the production fluid 28; receiving production fluid 28 produced from the subterranean region, the production fluid 28 including an initial proportion of hydrocarbons and an initial proportion of waterbased fluid; flowing the production fluid 28 into the inflow conduit 33; redirecting hydrocarbon fluid in the production fluid 28 to the first outflow conduit 30 by the oleophilic ribbon 62, to reduce the proportion of the water-based fluid in the first outflow conduit 30 relative to the initial proportion of the water-based fluid; redirecting water-based fluid of the production fluid 28 to the second outflow conduit 32 by the hydrophilic ribbon 60, to reduce the proportion of the hydrocarbons in the second outflow conduit 32 relative to the initial proportion of the hydrocarbons; and receiving the second portion of the production fluid 28 at a surface location. 12. The method 100 of claim 11, wherein the hydrophilic ribbon 60 is configured to attract water-based fluid and guide the water-based fluid in a viscous boundary layer toward the first outflow conduit 30, and the oleophilic ribbon 62 is configured to attract hydrocarbons and guide hydrocarbons in a viscous boundary layer toward the second outflow conduit 32. 13. The method 100 of claim 11, wherein at least part of the hydrophilic ribbon 60 is disposed proximate to an entrance of the second outflow conduit 32, and at least part of the oleophilic ribbon 62 is disposed proximate to an entrance of the first outflow conduit 30. 14. The method 100 of claim 11, wherein the hydrophilic ribbon 60 has a first end fixedly disposed relative to the inflow conduit 33 and a second end extending into a fluid volume, and the oleophilic ribbon 62 has a first end fixedly disposed relative to the inflow conduit 33 and a second end extending into a fluid volume. 15. The method 100 of claim 14, wherein the hydrophilic ribbon 60 is attached at the inflow conduit 33 and extends into the second outflow conduit 32, and the oleophilic ribbon 62 is attached at the inflow conduit 33 and extends into the first outflow conduit 30. 19
